reply_text: To clear things up: it's not "the venue" that decides how a crowd moves at an event, it's the concert production company, Live Nation. There's a whole production team they hire, whose literal job is to design not just for aesthetics, but for ingress/egress and the safety for thousands of innocent people at EVERY big festival and concert they produce. Travis Scott hired THEM to handle the logistics- it's not the job of the artist AT ALL- in fact it's the producer's job to shut down any creative ideas that could impede safety of the audience (pyrotechnics, crowd flow, evacuation plans, emergency weather plans, EMS access, etc.), including gatecrashing. Watch the doc to see the MANY ways Live Nation utterly failed to do their job. Throwing a festival is a huge responsibility- so much gets decided behind the scenes and whether it was Live Nation being cheap, passing blame, hiring amateurs or pure arrogance, they dropped the ball hard and those 10 kids died horrible, terrifying and senseless deaths & many more were traumatized on their watch.

video_title: Trainwreck: The Astroworld Tragedy | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: What happened at Astroworld would be nearly impossible in Poland. Our laws around mass events are extremely strict. Organizers must get official approval from the city, provide certified security personnel, limit the number of attendees based on venue size, and ensure clear, marked evacuation routes. Medical teams and ambulances must be present on-site. Every event requires a detailed safety and evacuation plan reviewed by police, fire services, and emergency medical authorities. An event this chaotic would never be allowed to happen here — it would be shut down before it even started.

reply_text: Yeah i was there except i was on the right side of the stage near the carnival. The design was flawed bc the chills stage was directly adjacent to the left side of the stage. I was at the barricade and after the 5th song it was impossible to breath. and people were dropping like flies. I backed up to get some air and was thankful for it. On the way out I saw multiple individuals receiving CPR. It was bad. I don't believe it was travis' fault but, there was this one moment when there was an emergency vehicle attempting to get to the crowd. Everyone could see it...even him but nothing was done to stop and the emergency vehicle was trampled and did much more harm than good with people standing on it and causing chaos. Overall, awful situation wish it didn't end the way it did.
